content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


May 14, 1991


Bill Baber, General Counsel
San Diego County Young Republicans
4167 46th Street
San Diego, CA 92105

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-91-226


Dear Mr. Baber:

You have requested advice on behalf of San Diego County Young Republicans (SDCYR) and California Young Republicans, Inc. concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act.¹

QUESTIONS

1. Are donated mailing expenses or the funds raised at a fundraiser held by SDCYR considered contributions?

2. Will SDCYR, or the California Young Republicans, Inc. be required to register and report as a "political committee?"

CONCLUSIONS

1. Based on the facts presented, the funds received for mailing expenses as well as the funds raised at the fundraiser are not contributions.

2. Based on the facts presented, SDCYR and the California Young Republicans, Inc. will not be required to register and report as a "political committee."


________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Bill Baber
A-91-226
Page 2


FACTS

SDCYR is a county chapter of the California Young
Republicans, Inc., a statewide Republican volunteer organization.
SDCYR plans to hold a fundraiser. State and local legislators
will provide some of the printing, stationery and postage for the
fundraiser. The funds raised at the fundraiser will be used only
for SDCYR fundraising expenses, administrative costs, membership
mailings, and meeting expenses. Funds raised will not be used to
make contributions to or otherwise support or oppose state or
local candidates or ballot measures, or for voter registration,
get-out-the-vote activities, or sample ballots.

ANALYSIS

Section 82015 defines "contribution" as:

...a payment, a forgiveness of a loan, a payment of a
loan by a third party, or an enforceable promise to
make a payment except to the extent that full and
adequate consideration is received unless it is clear
from the surrounding circumstances that it is not made
for political purposes....

Regulation 18215 (copy enclosed) clarifies that a payment is
made for "political purposes" if it is:

(1) For the purpose of influencing or
attempting to influence the action of the voters
for or against the nomination or election of a
candidate or candidates, or the qualification or
passage of any measure; or

(2) Received by or made at the behest of:....

(D) An organization formed or existing
primarily for political purposes as defined in
subsection (a)(1), including but not limited to
a political action committee established by any
membership organization, labor union or corporation.

Section 82013(a) defines "committee" as:

...any person or combination of persons who directly
or indirectly...

(a) Receives contributions totaling
one thousand ($1,000) or more in a calendar year.

You have indicated that although SDCYR will receive dona-
tions, it has not solicited and will not receive funds for the
purpose of making contributions or expenditures to support or
oppose candidates or ballot measures. According to the facts
presented, the activities of SDCYR and California Young
Republicans, Inc. will not result in the receipt of contributions,

Bill Baber
A-91-226
Page 3


and therefore will not qualify either organization as a
"recipient" committee pursuant to Section 82013(a) at this time.

     Please note that the Political Reform Act addresses only
those matters pertaining to California state and local candidates
and ballot measures.  You should contact the Federal Election Com-
mission regarding donations from candidates for federal office.

     If you have any further questions concerning this matter
please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [signature]

                                        By:  Sandra L. Taylor
                                             Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

sections.analysis:
Section 82015 defines "contribution" as:

...a payment, a forgiveness of a loan, a payment of a loan by a third party, or an enforceable promise to make a payment except to the extent that full and adequate consideration is received unless it is clear from the surrounding circumstances that it is not made for political purposes....

Regulation 18215 (copy enclosed) clarifies that a payment is made for "political purposes" if it is:

(1) For the purpose of influencing or attempting to influence the action of the voters for or against the nomination or election of a candidate or candidates, or the qualification or passage of any measure; or

(2) Received by or made at the behest of:...

(D) An organization formed or existing primarily for political purposes as defined in subsection (a)(1), including but not limited to a political action committee established by any membership organization, labor union or corporation.

Section 82013(a) defines "committee" as:

...any person or combination of persons who directly or indirectly...

(a) Receives contributions totaling one thousand ($1,000) or more in a calendar year.

You have indicated that although SDCYR will receive donations, it has not solicited and will not receive funds for the purpose of making contributions or expenditures to support or oppose candidates or ballot measures. According to the facts presented, the activities of SDCYR and California Young Republicans, Inc. will not result in the receipt of contributions,

and therefore will not qualify either organization as a "recipient" committee pursuant to Section 82013(a) at this time.

Please note that the Political Reform Act addresses only those matters pertaining to California state and local candidates and ballot measures. You should contact the Federal Election Commission regarding donations from candidates for federal office.
